extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a becomes true only when rule rec_a_from_conjunction fires, requiring diabetes AND peptic_ulcer AND renal_impairment. rec_drug_b becomes true when rule rec_b_from_trigger fires, requiring only diabetes. Rule A's condition is a strict superset of rule B's condition, so any input satisfying A's condition also satisfies B's. The two rules write to different outputs (a vs b), so the per-output priority mechanism (both priority 50) never causes one to suppress the other — they coexist. With diabetes=true, peptic_ulcer=true, renal_impairment=true: rec_a_from_conjunction fires setting rec_drug_a:=true, and rec_b_from_trigger fires setting rec_drug_b:=true. Both are jointly true, violating mutual exclusion. hyperkalemia is irrelevant (only affects rec_drug_c) and is set to false.
